behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because the failure is local template/lint-rule logic, not module handoff. Winner beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text "static prompt-template lint check"; this can be validated on an in-memory template string.
behaviors.test_description: Test the lint function/rule set with positive and negative template fixtures, including exact copied phase instructions from SKILL.md, and assert rejection occurs before runtime assembly.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because leakage can be introduced only when template + interpolation + fixture wiring combine. Integration beats System because this needs local module wiring only; no real network/subprocess/OS edge is required. Integration-brake checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly path with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert the final prompt text excludes phase-specific instruction snippets from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because the failure boundary is invocation wiring across loader + prompt builder + test runner. Integration beats System because local file-backed module interactions are sufficient; no external infra is inherently needed. Rejection-gate: Agentic is rejected for this behavior because it is about source-of-truth/provenance ("loads skill context ... rather than hard-coded prompt rules"), not model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation with real plugin/skill file loading enabled; assert loaded context is the one incorporated and that no hard-coded phase rules in test prompt drive pass/fail logic.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is harness-level composition (prompt text stability + skill variant swapping). Integration beats System because local filesystem fixture swapping is enough; no real external process/network needed. Integration-brake checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness runs over baseline vs degraded/missing SKILL.md fixtures while keeping the exact same user-style prompt literal, and assert the harness executes both variants without prompt edits.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration would cover harness wiring, but Agentic is required because correctness depends on real model output changing with skill-context degradation. Rejection-gate: Workflow is rejected because behavior text describes one behavioral outcome under the same prompt, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model calls with identical user-style prompt across baseline and degraded SKILL.md variants; assert structural behavior pass on baseline and fail on degraded variant, with identical test prompt text.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because ordering must be verified across capture + parser modules. Integration beats System because local artifact writing and call sequencing can be validated without external infra. Rejection-gate: Agentic is disqualified by behavior text focusing on capture ordering, not model-content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise harness path and assert an unparsed raw-output artifact is persisted first (including malformed JSON cases), then parsing/extraction proceeds.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because the core failure is assertion logic expressiveness on provided outputs (schema-path/field-placement checks). Winner beats System because no real infra is needed to prove keyword-only false positives are prevented. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified since behavior text targets assertion semantics, not runtime integration.
behaviors.test_description: Feed crafted outputs where keywords are present but structure is wrong, and outputs with correct structure but varied wording; assert structural checks fail/pass accordingly.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: System-level live execution alone is insufficient; Agentic wins because pass/fail depends on real LLM output variability and quality under the same prompt. Rejection-gate: Workflow is rejected because this behavior is about a single live generation evaluated by two assertion strategies, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model tests and compare outcomes of structural assertions vs keyword-only checks on the same raw outputs; require at least one real regression case caught only by structural assertions.
